title: Summer School
text:
NEMD for Fluids and Interfaces · 6–7 July 2026 · University of Edinburgh
The first NEMD summer school ran over the two days before NEMD 2026, with 39 participants. Ten lectures covered NEMD theory and methods for fluids and interfaces, paired with hands-on sessions running LAMMPS on the Cirrus supercomputer with EPCC support.

School website: nemd-school.org
Hands-on materials (LAMMPS + Python, open to all): github.com/Non-Equilibrium-Molecular-Dynamics/NEMD-Summer-School-2026


The 2026 summer school at the Nucleus, King's Buildings.

title: Non-Equilibrium Molecular Dynamics
text:
Non-Equilibrium Molecular Dynamics
    The community for molecular simulation of fluids driven away from equilibrium: flows, thermal gradients, and interfaces. Meeting since 2020, worldwide.

title: NEMD 2025, Osaka
text:
4th conference · 11–13 June 2025 · Osaka University Nakanoshima Center
The fourth meeting and the first outside the UK. Chaired by Yasutaka Yamaguchi (Osaka University), with vice chairs Takeshi Omori and Takuya Kuwahara (Osaka Metropolitan University), the meeting was supported by the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science as a UK-Japan collaborative initiative, with refreshments sponsored by Tokyo Electron.
Across three days the programme held 13 keynotes, 5 contributed talks, and 14 posters. Keynote speakers: Billy Todd, Peter Daivis, Edward Smith, Takuya Kuwahara, Ozgur Yazaydin, Xinyan Wang, Rohit Pillai, Lois Smith, Kenichiro Koshiyama, Cillian Cockrell, Karl Travis, Stephen Sanderson, and Derek Basset (Tokyo Electron US), whose industry keynote closed the series of talks.
More detail is on the NEMD 2025 conference site.

NEMD 2025 at Osaka University Nakanoshima Center.

title: NEMD 2021, online
text:
2nd meeting · 6 September 2021 · Online
The second meeting was held online during the pandemic, over Microsoft Teams, with speakers joining from Australia, China, India, Europe, and the US. It was organised as a collaboration between Matthew Borg (Edinburgh), James Ewen (Imperial), and Edward Smith (Brunel).
The book of abstracts (PDF) is archived by the UK Fluids Network, and every talk was recorded: each title below links straight to the video.

title: NEMD 2021, online
text:
Talks

Peter Daivis, “Energy and temperature in NEMD simulations”
Billy Todd, “Pumping of nano-confined water with rotating electric fields”
Jun Zhang, “Molecular Dynamics and Multiscale simulation of Droplet Wetting and Evaporation”
Karl Travis, “New Dolls tensor-based algorithm for studying liquid fragmentation and the tensile test for solids”
Patrick Ilg, “Surface rheology of polymers at the liquid-vapour interface”
Suman Chakraborty, “Evaporation of Saline Aqueous Nano-Droplets: The Coffee-Ring Effect and Beyond”
Paola Carbone, “Thermodynamic properties of the graphene/electrolyte interface”
Philip Camp, “Using non-equilibrium molecular-dynamics simulations to study problems in industry”
Alessio Lavino, “Surface topography effects on pool boiling via non-equilibrium molecular dynamics simulations”
Ozgur Yazaydin, “Concentration Gradient Driven Molecular Dynamics to Model Gas and Liquid Phase Separations in Membranes”
Jesper Hansen, “On nanoscale polarization”
Kaihang Shi, “Can we define a unique microscopic pressure in inhomogeneous fluids?”
Jagjeevan Bhamra, “Interfacial Bonding Controls Friction in Diamond-Rock Contacts”
Dimitrios Mathas, “Evaluation of methods for viscosity simulations of lubricants at different temperatures and pressures: a case study on PAO-2”
James Sprittles, “Noisy Interfacial Nanoflows”
Lorenzo Botto, “Graphene nanohydrodynamics: non-equilibrium MD simulation of graphene nanoplatelets suspended in sheared liquids”
Sergey Karabasov, “A thermostat-consistent fully coupled molecular dynamics – generalised fluctuating hydrodynamics model for non-equilibrium flows”
“An overview of the 2021 Snook prize”, offered by Bill and Carol Hoover

The full recordings are also available in three parts: part 1, part 2, part 3.

title: Conferences
text:
The NEMD conference series has met five times since 2020, in the UK, online, and in Japan. Each meeting has its own page with dates, organisers, and materials.

  
    
    2026 · EdinburghNEMD 2026The 5th and largest meeting yet: 5 keynotes, 24 talks, 15 posters, 76 delegates from 15 countries, and the first summer school.
  
  
    
    2025 · OsakaNEMD 2025The 4th meeting and the first outside the UK, hosted at Osaka University Nakanoshima Center.
  
  
    
    2024 · LondonNEMD 2024The 3rd meeting: two days of talks and poster sessions at Imperial College London.
  
  
    2nd
    2021 · OnlineNEMD 2021The online meeting, with full session recordings available on YouTube.
  
  
    1st
    2020 · LondonNEMD 2020The kick-off meeting at Brunel University London that started the series.

title: About
text: Non-equilibrium molecular dynamics (NEMD) is the study of fundamental fluid flow using molecular simulation: non-equilibrium because the system is driven away from thermodynamic equilibrium by wall motion, temperature and pressure gradients, or because it contains an interface such as a liquid-vapour coexistence.

title: About
text:
The community
NEMD started in 2020 as a special interest group of the UK Fluids Network, kicked off at a meeting at Brunel University London on 17 January 2020. Since then it has grown into an international conference series: five meetings across the UK, online, and Japan, with the largest, NEMD 2026 in Edinburgh, gathering 76 delegates from 15 countries alongside the first NEMD summer school.
The name has a longer history: NEMD workshops ran in the 2010s before the current series, and the field’s roots go back to the first molecular simulations of shear flow. A fuller history page is planned; if you have material from the earlier era, please get in touch.

title: About
text:
Get involved
Join the mailing list: email contact.nemd@gmail.com with the subject line “subscribe”.

title: NEMD 2020, London
text:
1st meeting · 17 January 2020 · Brunel University London
The kick-off meeting of the Non-Equilibrium Molecular Dynamics special interest group of the UK Fluids Network, organised by Edward Smith (Brunel University London) and James Ewen (Imperial College London). A one-day meeting of talks and discussion sessions that started the series.
Please see the programme and abstracts (PDF).

title: NEMD 2020, London
text:
Talks

Ian Ford, “Entropy production for non-Markovian stochastic dynamics”
Dimitrios Mathas, “An evaluation of Equilibrium Green-Kubo and Nonequilibrium Molecular Dynamics for viscosity simulations of lubricants at different temperatures and pressures”
Sritay Mistry, “Charge based rejection in Boron Nitride nanotubes”
James Ewen, “NEMD in Tribology - Methods and Applications” (slides)
Mike Allen, “Viscosities of Smectic-A Phases” (slides)
Fernando Bresme, “Thermal transport across soft and hard interface”
Josep Bonet Avalos, “Fluctuations in Generalised Dissipative Particle Dynamics”
Lev Kantorovitch, “Non-equilibrium MD based on Generalised Langevin Equation” (slides)
Karl Travis, “Non-equilibrium Molecular Dynamics: A powerful tool for gaining insight into fluid behavior”
Hongtao Zhang, “Effects of suspending Al nanoparticles on nucleation, microexplosion and combustion of a kerosene droplet: measurement and molecular dynamics simulation”
Carlos Ayestarán Latorre, “Surfactants Interactions with Engineering Surfaces: From Density Functional Theory to Molecular Dynamics”

No photos of this first meeting are in the archive. If you have some, please email contact.nemd@gmail.com.

title: NEMD 2024, London
text:
3rd conference · 13–14 June 2024 · Imperial College London
The third meeting: two days at Imperial College London with 8 invited talks, 17 contributed talks, 7 posters, and a conference dinner. The meeting was part funded by the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science as a joint Japanese-UK conference.
Invited speakers: Daniele Dini (Imperial College London), Fernando Bresme (Imperial College London), Marcello Sega (UCL), Yasutaka Yamaguchi (Osaka University), Shin-ichi Tsuda (Kyushu University), Peter Daivis (RMIT), Billy Todd (Swinburne University of Technology), and Kazumichi Kobayashi (Hokkaido University).
The book of abstracts (PDF) has the full programme; it is archived by the UK Fluids Network.

NEMD 2024 at Imperial College London.

title: NEMD 2026, Edinburgh
text:
5th conference · 8–10 July 2026 · The Nucleus, University of Edinburgh
The fifth meeting was the largest yet: 76 delegates from 15 countries across 4 continents, with 5 keynotes, 24 talks in total, and 15 posters presented through lightning-talk sessions. It was also the first meeting with a summer school, held on the two days before the conference.
For the full timetable, see the conference programme (PDF).
NEMD 2026 delegates at the Nucleus, King's Buildings.

title: NEMD 2026, Edinburgh
text:
Best Poster Award
Voted by the delegates, the award was shared by two posters:

Tiziano Cavalieri (University of Edinburgh), “A Non-Equilibrium Approach to Molecular Modelling of Gas Transport in a Mixed Matrix Membrane”
Taher Meydando (University of Edinburgh), “Determination of Nanobubble Zeta Potential via Non-Equilibrium Molecular Dynamics”


Poster prize winners Tiziano Cavalieri and Taher Meydando with committee member Eleonora Ricci.

title: NEMD 2026, Edinburgh
text:
Organisation
Organised by Rohit Pillai (chair), Duncan Dockar, Eleonora Ricci, and Santiago Romero-Vargas Castrillón (University of Edinburgh), Saikat Datta (Swansea University), and Edward Smith (Brunel University London).
Sponsored by Tokyo Electron Ltd. and CCP5. EPCC supported the summer school’s hands-on sessions.
